note:
波士顿动力公司今天上午宣布，继在美国销售之后，它将把Spot机器人带到一些新的市场。欧盟、英国和加拿大的感兴趣的公司现在可以购买技术先进的四足机器人。
Spot是波士顿动力公司的第一个商业化产品，它建立在25年来为DARPA和更远的地方推动机器人技术边界的基础上。在过去的几年里，该公司花费了大量的时间来展示“机器人”的各种不同工作的事实，从建筑测量到安全。
新冠肺炎疫情使人们越来越关注机器人行业，因为公司正在寻找一种方法，以提高自动化程度，保持照明，同时降低传播风险。波士顿动力公司已经利用这个机会让Spot在一些新的场景中工作，包括医院的远程操作。
波士顿动力公司注意到了一些第三方合作伙伴，包括Cognite、Energy Robotics、Clearpath Robotics和Reply，这些合作伙伴旨在未来帮助改进机器人的功能。该公司长期以来一直希望将机器人定位为一种平台，对各种不同的任务开放。这一设置减轻了Spot的创建者编写新功能的部分责任，并有望展示这款售价74500美元(价格因市场而异)的机器人的真正能力。
其他市场也将通过该公司的有限早期采用者计划进入现货市场。有关波士顿动力和Spot的更多信息，请务必收看我们下周在Disrupt2020上对新任首席执行官罗布·普莱特的采访。
note_en:
Boston Dynamics    this morning announced that it’s bringing Spot robot to a number of new markets, following  sales in the U.S.   Interested parties in the European Union, U.K. and Canada can now purchase the technologically advanced quadrupedal robot.
 Spot is Boston Dynamics’ first commercially available product, building on a quarter century of pushing the boundaries of robotics for DARPA and beyond. The company has spent a good deal of time over the past couple of years demonstrating a factotum of different jobs for the ‘bot, ranging from construction surveying to security.
 The COVID-19 pandemic has put increased focus on the robotics sector, as companies are looking for a way to increase automation to keep the lights on, while reducing the risk of transmission. Boston Dynamics has used the opportunity to put Spot to work in a number of new scenarios, including teleoperation in hospitals.
   Boston Dynamics notes a number of third-party partners designed to help improve the robot’s functionality going forward, including Cognite, Energy Robotics, Clearpath Robotics and Reply. The company has long look to position the robot as a kind of platform, open to a variety of different tasks. The setup takes some of the onus off of Spot’s creator to program new functionality and will hopefully demonstrate how capable the $74,500 robot (prices vary by market) can really be.
 Additional markets will also have access to Spot through the company’s limited Early Adopter Program. For more on Boston Dynamics and Spot, be sure to catch our interview with new CEO Rob Playter  next week at Disrupt 2020.
title: 波士顿动力公司的Spot机器人在欧洲和加拿大上市
title_en: Boston Dynamics’ Spot robot goes on sale in Europe and Canada

note:
建造量子计算机需要如此多的专业知识，因此我们现在看到一些同类最好的初创公司合作也就不足为奇了-这就是这两家公司现在合作的主要原因，也是为什么随着时间的推移，我们可能会看到更多这样的合作。
“(量子计算)的动机是我们可以从量子计算机获得的巨大计算能力，虽然它存在，但我们还没有实现它。我们还没有成熟的量子计算机，“量子机器公司的联合创始人兼首席执行官伊塔马尔·西文告诉我。
在很长一段时间里，人们的注意力都集中在根本挑战上，比如建造处理器，延长量子位的使用寿命，建造经典的控制硬件和软件等等。但真正的挑战是将所有这些整合在一起。
Sivan说：“这是一个多层次、多学科的挑战-一个极其复杂的挑战-实际上需要高度专注和高度专业化的主题密切合作，才能使量子计算机的梦想成为现实。”
Quantum Machines通过其Quantum编排平台提供集成的软件/硬件堆栈，该平台使用专门的经典硬件允许复杂算法在量子计算机上运行，而Q-CTRL提供量子控制基础设施软件-基本上是量子计算机的固件-帮助用户校准他们的处理器，减少噪音和随之而来的错误。
现在，Sivan和Q-CTRL创始人兼首席执行官迈克尔·J·比尔库克已经认识了一段时间(考虑到量子初创公司的生态系统毕竟没有那么大，这并不令人惊讶)，这无疑是有帮助的。但这两种技术也具有天然的互补性。
Biercuk说：“我们知道，每个团队用量子控制器解决的问题和量子机器正在建造的脉冲处理器-以及Q-CTRL正在建造的量子控制解决方案-之间有着奇妙的联系。真的，因为我有学术背景，我在这个领域花了20年的时间，我的学术团队建造量子计算硬件。”“从这一点和我提出的创办一家初创公司的动议中，我知道，在学术界非常常见的DIY方法，即一切都是自己做，完全垂直整合，在这个领域是行不通的。”
尽管合作是自然而然的选择，但Biercuk和Silvan都指出，在采取下一步行动之前，两家公司都必须在技术上达到成熟状态。考虑到该行业仍在不断变化，这种合作也不是排他性的，这是很有意义的。
note_en:
Building quantum computers takes so much specialized knowledge that it’s no surprise that we are now seeing some of the best-of-breed startups cooperate — and that’s pretty much why these two companies are now working together and why we’ll likely see more of these collaborations over time.
 “The motivation [for quantum computing] is this immense computational power that we could get from quantum computers and while it exists, we didn’t make it happen yet. We don’t have full-fledged quantum computers yet,”  Itamar Sivan, the co-founder and CEO of Quantum Machines, told me.
   For the longest time, the focus was on fundamental challenges, like building a processor, expanding the lifetimes of qubits, building the classical control hardware and software, etc. But the real challenge is bringing all of this together.
 “It’s a multi-layer, multidisciplinary challenge — an extremely complex challenge — that practically requires highly focused and highly specialized themes to closely collaborate and work together in order to make the dream of quantum computers a reality,” Sivan said.
 While Quantum Machines offers an integrated software/hardware stack through its Quantum Orchestration platform that uses specialized classical hardware to allow complex algorithms to run on quantum computers, Q-CTRL offers the quantum control infrastructure software — basically the firmware for quantum computers — that helps users calibrate their processors and reduce noise and the errors that come with it.
 Now it surely helped that Sivan and Q-CTRL founder and CEO Michael J. Biercuk already knew each other for a while (which is no surprise, given that the quantum startup ecosystem isn’t that big, after all). But the two technologies are also naturally complementary.
 “We knew that there was fantastic contact between the problems that each team was solving with the quantum controllers and the pulse processors that Quantum Machines was building — and the quantum control solutions that Q-CTRL was building — and really, because I have an academic background I spent the last 20 years in this field, my academic team builds quantum computing hardware,” Biercuk said. “I knew from the combination of that and my motion into founding a startup that the DIY approach of do everything yourself, with total vertical integration, that’s so common in academia, just does not work in this field.”
 While the partnership was a natural choice, though, both companies had to reach a state of maturity in their technologies before taking this next step, Biercuk and Silvan both noted. The partnership isn’t exclusive, either, which given how much the industry is still in flux, makes a lot of sense.
title: Q-CTRL和Quantum Machines联手加速量子计算
title_en: Q-CTRL and Quantum Machines team up to accelerate quantum computing

note:
为这一服务扩展提供资金的是以Oak HC/FT为首的投资者新一轮7500万美元的融资，Optum Ventures、TI Platform Management、Sound Ventures和Y Combinator参与其中。
新的牵头投资者Oak HC/FT的联合创始人兼董事总经理安妮·拉莫特(Annie Lamot)表示：“随着远程医疗报销的变化，它改变了直接面向消费者公司的轨迹。”“当我们与他们每一个人交谈时，他们似乎都在使用Truepill。”
随着其扩展到实验室检测领域，Truepill可以提供过去仅限于远程医生办公室的全套服务。随着更多的患者适应远程医疗，这些类型的选择将变得更具吸引力。
转向远程医疗也不仅仅是新进入者的事情。现任者还发现，他们需要提供与直接面向消费者竞争相同的医疗服务，特别是在优先事项转向基于价值的医疗服务，而不是报销端的服务收费的情况下-消费者开始要求直接支付端的更低成本选择。
“我认为这使医疗计划能够在有针对性的项目中提供更好的医疗服务，”拉蒙特说，他是医疗保健领域的长期投资者。
两位联合创始人奥马尔·阿夫里迪(Omar Afridi)和希德·维斯瓦纳坦(Sid Viswanathan。当时，阿夫里迪在西雅图附近的一家弗雷德·迈耶(Fred Meyer)担任药剂师配药。
最初，Truepill的增长来自于担任Hims、Ro、Nurx等公司的药剂师，以及其他直接面向消费者的医疗保健公司，专注于为需要脱发治疗、勃起功能障碍药物和节育的人提供选择性健康需求。
随着公司的发展，它的雄心也在增长。据Viswanathan称，到今年年底，Truepill预计将录得高达1.75亿美元的收入，这些收入将来自直接面向消费者公司、保险公司和医疗保健提供商的更均匀的客户组合。
“从我们的药房到实验室检测部件，我们所做的一切都贴上了白色标签。你可以去Teladoc使用这项服务。我们喜欢早点想的。80%的医疗保健将通过数字渠道进行。我们处于一个完美的位置，可以在这个领域建立平台公司，“维斯瓦纳坦说。
在家测试是该平台的关键组成部分。Truepill预计将在今年年底前推出，目前正在与实验室测试提供商合作，提供数百种家庭测试。该公司表示，将专注于治疗糖尿病、心脏病、慢性肾脏疾病等慢性疾病的测试。顺便说一句，这些领域吸引了投资者的极大兴趣，他们支持那些为消费者提供直接或数字治疗解决方案的公司，以治疗或帮助解决这些问题。
Viswanathan在一份声明中说：“要创造全面、有效的数字医疗体验，有三个基本支柱：具有广泛保险覆盖范围的药房、家庭实验室测试和远程医疗。”通过在我们的解决方案套装中增加诊断功能，我们将能够通过一个平台--Truepill向患者提供大规模的直接医疗保健。我们展望了80%的医疗保健是数字化的未来。有了建立在我们与API连接的基础设施基础上的诊断、远程保健和制药，Truepill将推动这一现实。“
note_en:
Financing this expansion of services is a new $75 million round of financing from investors led by Oak HC/FT, with participation from  Optum Ventures,   TI Platform Management, Sound Ventures and Y Combinator.
 “With the change in reimbursement for telemedicine, it changed the trajectory of the direct to consumer companies,” said Annie Lamot, the co-founder and managing director of new lead investors Oak HC/FT. “When we talked to every one of them they all seemed to be using  Truepill  .”
  With its expansion into lab testing, Truepill can provide a full suite of services that used to be confined to the doctor’s office remotely. As more patients adjust to remote delivery of care, these kinds of options will become more attractive.
 The move to telemedicine isn’t just something for new entrants either. Incumbents are also finding that they need to provide the same care as their direct to consumer competition, especially as the priority shifts to value-based care rather than fees for services on the reimbursement side — and consumers start demanding lower cost options on the direct pay side.
 “I think it enables health plans to provide better care in targeted programs,” said Lamont, a longtime investor in healthcare.
  The two co-founders, Umar Afridi and Sid Viswanathan met over  LinkedIn   where Viswanathan cold-emailed Afridi. At the time, Afridi was working as  a pharmacist filling prescriptions at a Fred Meyer near Seattle).
 Initially, Truepill’s growth came from acting as the pharmacist to companies like  Hims,    Ro,   Nurx, and other direct-to-consumer healthcare companies focused on serving the elective health needs of people who wanted hair loss treatments, erectile dysfunction medication, and birth control.
  As the company has grown, so have its ambitions. By the end of the year, Truepill expects to book up to $175 million in revenue, according to Viswanathan, and that revenue will come from a more evenly distributed mix of customers among direct to consumer companies, insurance companies, and healthcare providers.
 “Everything we do is white labeled from our pharmacy to the lab testing component. You can go to teladoc and use that service. What we like to think early. 80 percent of healthcare is going to happen on a digital channel.. We’re in a perfect position to build the platform company in that space,” Viswanathan said.
 At-home testing is a critical component of that platform. Expected to launch before the end of the year, Truepill is working with lab testing providers to offer hundreds of at-home tests. The company said it will focus on tests to manage chronic conditions like diabetes, heart disease, chronic kidney disease. Incidentally these are areas which have attracted a lot of interest from investors who are backing companies that provide direct to consumer or digital therapeutic solutions to treat or help address these conditions.
 “To create a comprehensive, effective digital healthcare experience, there are three essential pillars: pharmacy with extensive insurance coverage, at-home lab testing and telehealth,” said Viswanathan, in a statement. “By adding diagnostics to our suite of solutions, we’ll be able to deliver direct-to-patient healthcare at scale through one platform – Truepill. We envision a future where 80% of healthcare is digital. With diagnostics, telehealth and pharmacy built on our foundation of API-connected infrastructure, Truepill will power that reality.”
title: Truepill接近7500万美元的新现金，计划提供家庭测试服务，因为它的年收入接近1.75亿美元
title_en: Closing on $75 million in new cash, Truepill plans at-home testing service as it nears $175 million in annual revenue

note:
Sana Benefits是一家为小企业提供自筹资金的保险计划管理公司，该公司表示，它在最近一轮融资中筹集了2,080万美元，希望将所有最新的初创医疗福利提供商整合到一个方便的小企业套餐中。
自筹资金的保险计划是由公司设立的，用于自掏腰包支付员工的医疗保健费用，而且通常更便宜，因为雇主可以挑选他们提供的服务。
根据Sana Health联合创始人威尔·杨(Will Young)的说法，大多数公司最终花费太多，因为他们从联合医疗(United Healthcare)、国歌蓝十字蓝盾(Anhim Blue Cross Blue Shield)、安泰(Aetna)、信诺(Cigna)或Humana等大型保险公司购买现成的保险计划。
该公司兜售与初创公司的合作伙伴关系，如Beam Dental提供牙科保险，PlushCare提供远程医疗，Calm and Ginger.io提供心理健康，ClassPass提供身体健身，Maven Clinic提供产妇护理。
它的推销吸引了Gigafund、Trust Ventures和Mark Vc的注意，这三家公司为该公司2080万美元的首轮融资提供了支持。
Gigafund的管理合伙人Stephen Oskoui在一份声明中表示：“Sana的医疗保险颠覆性模式使小企业既能削减成本，又能提高员工福利。”“我们相信，只有像萨那这样的双赢解决方案才能在医疗危机中产生真正的影响。”
员工能得到什么？萨那的计划范围从提供4000美元的免赔额和6650美元的自掏腰包最高支付6650美元的医疗保险，到0美元的个人自掏腰包最高支出为1250美元的免赔额计划。
杨说：“我们为我们的客户节省了20%的费用，这是他们在传统计划中可以获得的。”“从我们客户公司的角度来看。自保从技术上讲，是指公司提供保险，并自己购买保险。“
note_en:
Sana Benefits, a manager of self-funded insurance plans for small businesses, said it has raised $20.8 million in a recent round of funding as it looks to roll up all of the latest startup health benefit providers into a convenient package for small businesses.
 Self-financed insurance plans are set up by companies to pay out of pocket for their employees’ health care and are typically cheaper, because employers can pick and choose which services they offer.
 According to Sana Health co-founder Will Young, most companies wind up spending too much because they buy off-the-shelf plans from the big insurance companies like United Healthcare,  Anthem   Blue Cross Blue Shield, Aetna, Cigna or Humana.
  The company touts partnerships with startups like Beam Dental for dental coverage, PlushCare for telemedicine, Calm and Ginger.io for mental wellness,  ClassPass   for physical fitness, and  Maven Clinic   for maternity care.
 Its pitch attracted the attention of  Gigafund,   Trust Ventures and mark vc, which came in to back the company’s $20.8 million series A round.
 “Sana’s disruptive model for health insurance empowers small businesses to both cut costs and improve employee benefits,” said  Stephen Oskoui,   Managing Partner of Gigafund, in a statement. “We believe that only a win-win solution like Sana can make a real dent in the healthcare crisis.”
 What do employees get? Sana’s plans range from health insurance offerings with a $4,000 deductible and $6,650 in out of pocket maximum payments of $6,650 to a $0 deductible plan with a $1,250 out of pocket maximum expense for individuals.
 “We save our customers 20 percent on what they would get on a traditional plan,” according to Young. “From the perspective of our client company. Self insured means technically the company is offering insurance and buying insurance itself.”
title: 汇总最新的创业健康保险产品，Sana Benefits筹集了2080万美元
title_en: Rolling up the latest in startup health insurance offerings, Sana Benefits raises $20.8 million

note:
Xbox系列X是微软Xbox One的高端继任者，它包括一系列非常令人印象深刻的硬件规格，包括8核定制CPU，16 GB RAM，12万亿次浮点处理能力的GPU，用于超快加载和传输速度的1TB NVMe固态硬盘，以及在60和120 FPS下支持高达4K的分辨率。S系列是其功耗较低的同类产品，去掉了物理磁盘驱动器，并提供了更低的规格，包括仅支持60fps的1440p分辨率，以及更小的512 GB NVMe固态硬盘。不过，S系列将使用与X系列相同的CPU。微软显然希望通过其新一代游戏机同时吸引廉价和高端游戏玩家。
本周早些时候，Xbox Series S的官方预告片和预告片以及定价信息泄露。微软随后证实了这些细节，并发布了一款功能较弱、非常紧凑的游戏机的发布预告片，但直到今天确认之前，我们都不确定Xbox Series X是否会在同一天发布。此外，今天首次披露了更高端硬件的499美元标价。
Xbox系列X还可以通过微软的Xbox All Access订阅获得，价格为每月34.99美元，为期两年。S系列也将以分期付款的方式提供，同样的24个月期间，每月24.99美元。这些订阅还包括Xbox Game Pass旗舰版，在您的硬件付款期间，您可以使用微软的订阅游戏库。
note_en:
The Xbox Series X is Microsoft’s higher-end successor to the Xbox One, and it includes a range of very impressive hardware specs including an 8-core custom CPU, 16GB of RAM, a GPU with 12 teraflops of processing power, a 1 TB NVMe SSD for super fast load and transfer speeds, and support for up to 4K resolution at both 60 and 120 FPS. The Series S is its lower-powered sibling, taking out the physical disc drive and offering lower specs including support for only up to 1440p resolution at 60fps, along with a smaller 512GB NVMe SSD. The Series S will use the same CPU as the Series X, however. Microsoft is clearly hoping to appeal to both budget and premium gamers with its new generation of consoles.
 Earlier this week, the  official teaser image and trailer for the Xbox Series S leaked, along with pricing information. Microsoft subsequently confirmed those details and released a launch trailer for the less powerful, and very compact console, but we didn’t know for sure whether the Xbox Series X would share the same launch day until today’s confirmation. Also, the $499 price tag of the more premium hardware was revealed for the first time today.
   The Xbox Series X will also be available through Microsoft’s Xbox All Access subscription, at a price of $34.99 per month, over a two-year period. The Series S will also be available on an instalment payment basis, at $24.99 per month per the same 24-month span. Those subscriptions also include Xbox Game Pass Ultimate, providing access to Microsoft’s library of subscription games during the term of your hardware payments.
title: 微软确认Xbox Series X售价499美元11月10日到货9月22日开始预购
title_en: Microsoft confirms $499 Xbox Series X arrives November 10, pre-orders begin September 22

note:

note_en:
If you are already an Extra Crunch member, scroll down to the bottom of the post for directions on how to claim the credits. If you are not yet a member, keep reading.
  AWS ( Amazon   Web Services) is the premier service for your application hosting needs, and we want to make sure our community is well-resourced to build. We understand that hosting and infrastructure costs can be a major hurdle for tech startups, and we’re hoping that this offer will help better support your team.
    Not all companies are eligible for AWS Activate credits. To apply to AWS Activate Founders, your startup must meet the following criteria:
 Startup must be self-funded, unbacked or bootstrapped — no institutional funding or affiliation with an Activate Provider
   Extra Crunch is a membership program from TechCrunch that helps you spot technology trends and opportunities, build better startups and stay connected. It features thousands of articles, including weekly investor surveys, daily market analysis and expert interviews on fundraising, growth, monetization and other work topics.
 Extra Crunch also features Extra Crunch Live Q&A sessions with startup experts, a  20% discount on TechCrunch events, access to Partner Perks and better ways to use and navigate TechCrunch.com.
   The Extra Crunch  Partner Perks program is a great way for our annual members to save a few bucks on software costs. Since launching the program last fall, we’ve added more than a dozen new perks, including discounts on  Crunchbase,  DocSend and more. To see a full list of Partner Perks, head  here.
title: 提醒：额外的Crunch会员可获得价值1,000美元的AWS激活积分
title_en: Reminder: $1,000 in AWS Activate credits available for Extra Crunch members

note:
但这些年来，创建一家硬件公司是否变得更容易了呢？还是2020年本身就引入了所有新的挑战？
在Disrupt2020-我们有史以来第一次完全虚拟的Disrupt！-将有两个人加入我们的行列，他们对让事情变成现实的一切都有丰富的洞察力：Y Combinator的埃里克·米吉科夫斯基(Eric Migicovsky)和蚕蛹云(Chrysalis Cloud)的凯特·惠特科姆(Kate Whitcomb)。
作为Pebble的创始人，埃里克·米吉科夫斯基(Eric Migicovsky)领导的团队推出了首批值得你手腕上空间的智能手表。在2016年被Fitbit收购(可悲的是，被关闭)之前，该公司击败了世界上最大的苹果和Android手表，出货量超过200万块。几年后，Pebble仍然保持着Kickstarter有史以来资金最多的五个活动中的两个的记录。今天，埃里克是Y Combinator的合伙人，在那里他帮助了无数处于早期阶段的初创公司规划了未来的道路，并领导了这家加速器的在线创业学校(Online Startup School)项目。
2020年，凯特·惠特科姆(Kate Whitcomb)联合创立了Chrysalis Cloud，这是一个旨在帮助公司以更便宜、更低延迟的方式收集和分析流媒体数据的平台，此前该平台是从智能婴儿监视器制造商茧健康(Cocoon Health)剥离出来的。在此之前，凯特是HAX硬件加速器的合伙人，在那里她帮助来自世界各地的数百家初创企业将他们的产品概念带到了世界各地-甚至在此之前，她还领导了Target的消费物联网团队的增长和创新。她还在这里为TechCrunch写了关于如何让你的硬件出现在零售商面前的文章！
这两位专家对任何希望在2020年创办硬件公司的人有什么建议？他们现在会开始建造新的硬件吗？他们认为这样做最大的潜在挑战是什么？我很高兴能找到答案。埃里克和凯特都花了几年时间把想法变成现实，也花了几年时间帮助别人做同样的事情。无论您是在硬件初创公司工作还是投资硬件公司，您都不会想错过它的…。而且，今年，你甚至不需要离开家就能捕捉到它的每一个点点滴滴。
正如我们早在5月份宣布的那样，Disrupt 2020将是我们第一次完全虚拟地做这个节目…。这将是一场多么精彩的演出。我们甚至还没有宣布完整的议程，这已经是令人惊叹的事情了。查尔斯·哈德森！罗洛夫·博塔！德鲁·休斯顿，迈克尔·西贝尔，还有詹妮弗·杜德娜！
当然，还有创业战场(Startup Battlefield)--Dropbox、Fitbit、Mint等众多初创企业首次亮相的初创企业竞赛。
“扰乱2020”将于9月14日至9月18日举行。如果你想以参会者的身份参观，Digital Pro Pass起价345美元；如果你想在Startup Alley展出，请获得您的Digital Startup Alley Exhibitor套餐。或者，如果你只是想赶上这次会议，你可以只花45美元就可以获得一张数字通行证。同时，你可以在这里找到所有这些信息。希望在那里见到你！
note_en:
But has building a hardware company gotten any easier over the years? Or has 2020 introduced all new challenges of its own?
 At  Disrupt 2020 — our first ever entirely virtual Disrupt! — we’ll be joined by two people who have tons of insight into everything that goes into making something real: Eric Migicovsky of Y Combinator and Kate Whitcomb of Chrysalis Cloud.
  As the founder of Pebble, Eric Migicovsky led the team behind the first smartwatches worthy of the space on your wrist. Beating the Apple and Android watches of the world to the punch by years, the company shipped over 2 million watches before being acquired (and, sadly, shutdown) by Fitbit in 2016. Years later, Pebble still holds the records for two of the top five  most funded Kickstarter campaigns ever. Today Eric is a partner at Y Combinator, where he has helped countless early-stage startups map out the road ahead and leads the accelerator’s online  Startup School program.
 In 2020 Kate Whitcomb co-founded  Chrysalis Cloud, a platform meant to help companies collect and analyze streaming data in a cheaper, lower latency way, after spinning it out of smart baby monitor maker Cocoon Health. Before that Kate was a partner at the HAX hardware accelerator, where she helped hundreds of startups from around the world bring their product concepts into the world — and even before that, she led Growth and Innovation for Target’s Consumer IoT team. She also wrote about how to get your hardware in front of retailers  for TechCrunch here!
 What advice do these two experts have for anyone looking to start a hardware company in 2020? Would they start building new hardware right now, and what do they see as the biggest potential challenges in doing so? I’m excited to find out. Both Eric and Kate spent years bringing ideas into reality and spent years helping others do the same. Whether you’re working on a hardware startup or investing in hardware companies, you won’t want to miss it… and, this year, you won’t even have to leave your house to catch every bit of it.
 As we announced  back in May,  Disrupt 2020 will be the first time we’ve done this show entirely virtually… and what a show it’ll be. We haven’t even announced the full agenda yet, and  it’s already amazing. Charles Hudson! Roelof Botha! Drew Houston, Michael Seibel, and Jennifer Doudna!
 And then, of course, there’s the Startup Battlefield — the startup competition where Dropbox, Fitbit, Mint, and so many others made their big debuts.
 Disrupt 2020 will run from September 14th to September 18th. If you want to check it out as an attendee, the  Digital Pro Pass starts at $345; if you want to exhibit in the Startup Alley, get your  Digital Startup Alley Exhibitor Package. Or if you just want to catch this session, you can  get a Digital Pass for just $45. meanwhile, you can find all of that info right here. Hope to see you there!
title: 听Y Combinator的Eric Migicovsky和Chrysalis Cloud的Kate Whitcomb在Disrupt2020上谈论硬件
title_en: Hear Y Combinator’s Eric Migicovsky and Chrysalis Cloud’s Kate Whitcomb talk hardware at Disrupt 2020

note:
如果你知道一件关于Mint Mobile的事情，那很可能是它的所有者是披萨餐厅(Pizza Place)出身的皮卡丘配音(Pikachu Voice)瑞安·雷诺兹(Ryan Reynolds)。这位演员一直在为自己建立一个很好的投资组合，从2018年美国金酒航空公司(Aviation American Gin Company)的一部分开始，去年持有铸币厂(Mint)的所有权股份。
该公司是一家移动虚拟网络运营商，专门从事建立在T-Mobile网络之上的廉价预付费电话计划。今天，它为美国推出了一项新的无限制通话和短信计划，费用为30美元。该计划包括35 GB的5G或LTE数据，具体取决于您所在地区的信号强度。
从那时起，定价变得更加复杂-每月30美元是为期三个月的介绍性报价，使用的是按年计算的每月定价。如果您在前三个月后感到满意，您可以续订三个月，每月40美元，续订六个月，每月35美元，或者全年续订，每月30美元。
为了纪念这一时刻，雷诺兹主演了三个电视广告，其中包括年度最佳教师罗德尼·罗宾逊(Rodney Robinson)、保罗·里维尔(Paul Revere)的后裔/船长/钓鱼向导艾弗里·里维尔(Avery Revere)和加拿大演员里克·莫拉尼斯(Rick Moranis)。这位SCTV的校友几十年来一直明显地避开好莱坞，尽管有许多高调的特许经营权重聚，比如捉鬼敢死队。在一个有趣而滑稽的小地方，出于某种原因放在田野里。
“和许多人一样，在过去的十年里，我一直怀念在电影中看到里克，所以我恳求他在”铸币厂“中重新露面，”雷诺兹在谈到现场时说。“除了今年很奇怪之外，真的没有什么很好的理由，我想我们都可以使用更多的里克·莫拉尼斯(Rick Moranis)。”当然可以。
莫拉尼斯将在与“甜心，我缩水的孩子”中断23年后重返电影界，重新开机，缩水。
note_en:
If you know one thing about  Mint   Mobile, it’s probably the fact  that it’s owned by Pizza Place guy turned Pikachu voice, Ryan Reynolds. The actor’s been building a nice investment portfolio for himself, beginning with a piece of the Aviation American Gin company in 2018 and an ownership stake in Mint last year.
 The company is a mobile virtual network operator, specializing in cheap prepaid phone plans built on top of  T-Mobile   network. Today it launched a new unlimited talk and text plan for the U.S. for $30. The plan includes either   35GB of 5G or LTE data,   depending on the strength of the signal in your area.
   The pricing gets more complicated from there — the $30 a month is an introductory offer for three months, using the annual per month pricing. If you’re satisfied after the first three months, you can renew for another three months at $40 a month, six months at $35 a month or go in for the full year to stay at $30 a month.
  To mark the occasion, Reynolds is starring in a trio of TV spots, featuring Teacher of the Year Rodney Robinson, Paul Revere descendant/Captain/fishing guide Avery Revere and fellow Canadian actor, Rick Moranis. The SCTV alum has notably eschewed Hollywood for a couple of decades now, in spite of a number of high profile reunions of franchises like Ghostbusters. In a fun and goofy little spot, set in a field for some reason.
  “Like many, I’ve missed seeing Rick in movies for the past decade, so I pretty much begged him to reemerge for Mint,” Reynolds says of the spot. “There’s really no good reason for it expect this year has been weird and I thought we could all use more Rick Moranis.” Fair enough.
 Moranis is set to return to films after a 23-year hiatus with the Honey, I Shrunk the Kids reboot, Shrunk.
title: 里克·莫拉尼斯(Rick Moranis)打破表演间歇30秒，推出Mint每月30美元的无限制计划
title_en: Rick Moranis breaks acting hiatus for 30 seconds to launch Mint’s $30 a month unlimited plan

note:
上周，我与一位风投谈到了私营SaaS公司的新基准结果，令我惊讶的是，他说软件初创公司不一定要增长100%才能在今天的市场上获得融资。考虑到我从其他风险资本家那里听到的关于他们的投资组合中有这么多发现了新冠肺炎的增长突起，这些观点感觉不协调。
该交易所探索初创企业、市场和资金。你可以每天早上在Extra Crunch上阅读它，或者每周六获得Exchange时事通讯。
初创企业希望在大流行前以100%以上的速度增长，自那以来，一些初创企业已经加速增长。那么，一家年增长率低于三位数的初创公司怎么会有吸引力呢？再加上Zoom令人印象深刻的收益业绩，以及本财报周期早些时候的一些警告信号，表明云增长并没有像预期的那样快，这让人感觉减少了。
今天上午阅读该公司提交给美国证券交易委员会的与收益相关的文件时，很难错过斯拉克关于新冠肺炎的笔记。这家企业通信公司描述了大流行带来的早期好处，以及与其经济影响相关的挥之不去的痛苦。简而言之，与软件相关的COVID-BUMP可能会在中短期内留下后遗症。
这有助于我们理解为什么一家软件初创公司在2020年可能会在没有100%增长率的情况下具有风投吸引力。也许比通常所说的更多的SaaS和云初创公司在苦苦挣扎，这意味着如果其他指标闪烁着绿色，收入增长放缓是可以接受的。
为了了解你最喜欢的初创公司可能会发生什么，让我们从好消息开始，先梳理一下斯拉克与新冠肺炎相关的商业笔记，然后再转向我之前提到的坏消息-以及更坏的消息。
note_en:
I spoke with a VC last week about what the new benchmark results are for private SaaS companies, and to my surprise, he said software startups don’t have to grow at 100% to be fundable in today’s market. Given what I’d heard from other venture capitalists about how so much of their portfolios had found a COVID-19 growth bump, the perspectives felt incongruous.
  The Exchange explores startups, markets and money. You can read it  every morning on Extra Crunch, or get  The Exchange newsletter every Saturday.
  Startups wanted to grow at a pace of more than 100% pre-pandemic, and some have accelerated since. So how could a startup growing less than three figures yearly be attractive? Throw  in Zoom’s impressive earnings results and  some warning signs from earlier this earnings cycle that cloud growth hasn’t wound up being quite as fast as expected felt diminished.
  Reading the  company’s SEC filing related to earnings this morning, it’s hard to miss Slack’s notes about COVID-19. The enterprise communications company describes early benefits from the pandemic, along with lingering pain associated with its economic impacts. In short, the software-related COVID-bump could wind up leaving a hangover in the short- to medium-term.
 This helps us understand why a software startup could be VC-attractive in 2020 without a 100% growth rate. Perhaps more SaaS and cloud startups than have been generally told are struggling, which means slower revenue expansion is palatable provided that other indicators are flashing green.
 To understand what could be happening to your favorite startup, let’s tease apart Slack’s COVID-19-related business notes, starting with the good news, before turning to what I’ve penciled in as the bad news — and the even badder tidings.
title: Sack的收益详细说明了新冠肺炎是如何帮助和阻碍云增长的
title_en: Slack’s earnings detail how COVID-19 is both a help and a hindrance to cloud growth

note:
此次收购的财务条款没有披露。Socialbakers首席执行官尤瓦尔·本-伊扎克(Yuval Ben-Itzhak)将成为合并后公司的Socialbakers总裁，他通过电子邮件告诉我，整个Socialbakers团队也将加入，从而形成一个拥有600多名员工和1亿美元年经常性收入的合并组织。
Socialbakers是第一波社交分析浪潮中最后几个独立的参与者之一。据Crunchbase称，该公司成立于2008年，总部位于布拉格，从包括Earlybird Venture Capital和Index Ventures在内的投资者那里总共筹集了3400万美元的资金。全球有超过25000个品牌在使用它。
与此同时，精明已有25年的历史，专注于统一客户数据。Ben-Itzhak表示，通过收购Socialbakers，Astute将能够添加以社交媒体为重点的功能，如受众洞察、内容规划、影响力营销和广告分析。
他说：“Socialbakers和Astute已经在企业领域共享了数十个共同的品牌客户。”“事实上，这就是收购谈判的来龙去脉。平台整合过程已经开始，预计将持续到第四季度。“。
在一份声明中，Astute首席执行官马克·扎布兰也强调了由此产生的平台的全面性。
扎布兰说：“客户服务、客户体验和营销之间的界限变得越来越模糊，给公司带来了真正的挑战。”将Socialbakers的市场领先的社交媒体营销能力与Astute的客户互动套装相结合，不仅有助于我们的客户更有效地应对这一挑战，而且标志着Astute成为首席客户官成功所需的端对端客户互动平台之路上的一个重要里程碑。“
note_en:
The financial terms of the acquisition were not disclosed.  Socialbakers   CEO Yuval Ben-Itzhak will become president of Socialbakers for the combined company, and he told me via email that the entire Socialbakers team will be joining as well, resulting in a combined organization with more than 600 employees and $100 million in annual recurring revenue.
 Socialbakers was one of the last independent players from the first wave of social analytics. Founded in 2008 and based in Prague, the company raised a total of $34 million in funding,  according to Crunchbase, from investors including Earlybird Venture Capital and Index Ventures. And it’s used by more than 2,5000 brands globally.
  Astute, meanwhile, has been around for 25 years, and focuses on unifying customer data. Ben-Itzhak said that by acquiring Socialbakers, Astute will be able to add social media-focused features like audience insights, content planning, influencer marketing and ad analytics.
 “Socialbakers and Astute are already sharing dozens of mutual brand customers in the enterprise segment,” he said. “This is, in fact, how the acquisition talks came about. The platform integration process has already started and is expected to continue through Q4.”
 In a statement, Astute CEO Mark Zablan also emphasized the comprehensiveness of the resulting platform.
 “The lines between customer care, customer experience, and marketing have become increasingly blurred, presenting real challenges for companies,” Zablan said. “Combining the market-leading social media marketing capabilities of Socialbakers with Astute’s engagement suite not only helps our customers tackle this challenge more effectively, but also marks a major milestone along Astute’s journey towards becoming the end-to-end customer engagement platform that the Chief Customer Officer needs to succeed.”
title: Socialbakers被客户接洽公司Astute收购
title_en: Socialbakers acquired by customer engagement company Astute

note_en: [contact-form to=’author@nypost.com’ subject=’NYPost.com Feedback’][contact-field label=’Name’ type=’name’ required=’1’/][contact-field label=’Email’ type=’email’ required=’1’/][contact-field label=’Comment’ type=’textarea’ required=’1’/][/contact-form]
title: Facebook将在2020年大选前向用户支付注销费用
title_en: Facebook Will Pay Users To Log Off Before 2020 Election

note:
苹果改变了对智能应用程序横幅的处理方式，当用户打开由News+Publisher托管的网页时，该横幅会将用户指向Apple News应用程序，而不是第三方应用程序。
具体地说，智能应用程序的横幅现在会提示用户在Apple News应用程序中打开故事，至少对于目前使用API的News+合作伙伴是这样。正如iOS13中实现的那样，Smart App Banner框架允许开发者提示用户从App Store下载他们的第三方应用程序，或者从Safari打开相应的链接。
这一变化是在9月1日发布的最新iOS14测试版中做出的。3.。
在最新的iOS 14测试版中，智能应用横幅API或Safari似乎会检测到News+Partner Web域，从而触发显示修改后的促销横幅。受影响页面上的智能应用程序横幅代码没有改变，这表明Safari处理出版商URL的方式发生了变化。
如前所述，新横幅只出现在参与Apple News+的出版物上。开发者也许能够控制向用户显示哪个横幅--苹果新闻还是应用商店下载--尽管这一点目前还不清楚。
横幅行为是iOS14和MacOS Big Sur中一项新功能的补充，当用户点击News+Publisher链接时，该功能会自动将用户重定向到Apple News应用程序。苹果公司表示，这项功能是为了方便用户而设计的，因为Apple News+订阅不允许用户绕过出版商网站的付费墙。然而，一些出版商称这一新方向是阴暗的。
AppleInsider与会员有合作关系，通过会员链接购买的产品可能会赚取佣金。这些合作关系不会影响我们的编辑内容。
note_en:
Apple has made a change to the handling of Smart App Banners that points users to the  Apple News app instead of a third-party app when they open a webpage hosted by a News+ publisher.
  Specifically, Smart App Banners now prompt users to open stories in the Apple News app, at least for News+ partners currently using the API. As implemented in iOS 13, the Smart App Banner framework allows developers to prompt users to download their third-party apps from the App Store, or to open a corresponding link from Safari.
 The change was made in the latest  iOS 14 beta, which was  released on Sept. 3.
  In the most up-to-date iOS 14 beta, the Smart App Banner API or Safari appears to detect News+ partner web domains, which triggers display of the modified promotional banner. Smart App Banner code on affected pages is unchanged, suggesting that it's a change to the way Safari handles publisher URLs.
 As mentioned, the new banner only shows up for publications that participate in Apple News+. Developers may be able to control which banner appears to users — Apple News or App Store download — though that isn't clear at this point.
 The banner behavior is in addition to a new feature in iOS 14 and  macOS Big Sur that  automatically redirects users to the Apple News app when they click on a News+ publisher link. Apple says the feature is designed for user convenience, since an Apple News+ subscription doesn't allow users to bypass publisher website paywalls. Some publishers have called the new direction " shady," however.
    AppleInsider has affiliate partnerships and may earn commission on products purchased through affiliate links. These partnerships do not influence our editorial content.
title: 在最新的iOS14测试版中，当用户打开News+合作伙伴的网页时，智能应用程序横幅会提示用户在新闻应用程序中打开故事，而不是出版商应用程序
title_en: In the latest iOS 14 beta, Smart App Banners prompt users to open stories in the News app when they open webpages of a News+ partner, instead of publisher apps

note:
音乐即将成为堡垒之夜心中更大的一部分。今天，史诗将宣布一场新的、为期三周的系列音乐会，将在游戏的皇家派对岛上举行。它以多米尼克·菲克(Dominic Fike)于9月12日的现场表演拉开序幕，并将在洛杉矶一家专门为游戏内音乐会建造的全新演播室播出。这一切都是一个更大的计划的一部分，即把堡垒之夜的虚拟舞台变成音乐家的重要场所。
堡垒之夜的全球合作负责人内特·南泽解释说：“这是一个巡回演出。”“如果你是在巡演，你想停在堡垒之夜的舞台上。这是一种独一无二的方式，可以让观众看到你可能没有通过其他方式接触到的观众。“。
“如果你是在巡演，你想停在‘堡垒之夜’的舞台上。”
EPIC已经为此积累了一段时间。早在4月份，开发商就首次推出了Party Royale-堡垒之夜内部的一个独立的、无暴力的岛屿，它的特色包括一个音乐会舞台和一个剧院空间，以及其他景点。到目前为止，像狄普洛、史蒂夫·青木、戴德茂5号和永津健史这样的人已经表演了音乐，而这个剧院已经被用来放映Tenet的预告片，史诗的嘲弄苹果的短片“1980-堡垒之夜”，甚至还有一场关于美国种族的讨论。南泽说：“他们的表现真的很好，我们看到数以百万计的人来到这里，参加到目前为止在皇家派对上发生的这些不同的表演。”“因此，我们一直在关注这一点，并想出如何才能更频繁地做到这一点。”
为了做到这一点，Etic在洛杉矶建造了一个它所说的最先进的工作室空间。它有一个巨大的LED墙和地板，以及可以远程操作的机器人摄像头。(南泽说，大部分空间都是围绕COVID协议设计的。“我们已经仔细考虑过这一切了。”)。虽然过去的活动很成功，但也主要局限于DJ在自己家里表演。工作室的目的是让表演变得更大、更令人印象深刻，从Fike开始。南泽说：“这将是一场完整的实况转播音乐会。”“他将和他的乐队一起演奏，他将第一次现场演奏他的新音乐。”
在未来，Etic的团队希望利用这个舞台来利用混合和增强现实效果。(“史诗”还制造了虚幻引擎，可以用来构建这类体验，这是很有帮助的。)。南泽还说，“史诗报”正在考虑延长这些活动的时间。许多早期的皇家派对演唱会持续了10-15分钟，但随着场地的演变，情况可能并非如此。他解释说：“你可能会看到40-45分钟，可能是一个小时，对于一些目录更大的行为，可能会更多。”
最终目标是将Fornite的虚拟场地变成音乐界不可或缺的一部分。“我们认为这是一个艺术家的平台，”南泽说。“类似于如果你是一名艺术家，你有一张新专辑即将发行，你可能会在法伦或周六夜现场，或类似的平台上演奏。我们认为这是一个类似的平台，你可以在3.5亿多名堡垒之夜球员面前做到这一点。“。
这一系列音乐会的存在预计不会对堡垒之夜的大型音乐活动产生影响，比如马什梅洛和特拉维斯·斯科特的那些活动。这些吸引了数千万玩家，但它们也需要很长时间才能组合在一起-两项赛事之间有整整一年的时间。与此同时，皇室派对演唱会的目的是规模较小的演出，有助于填补这些长期的空白。“我想我们在未来也会继续举办令人惊叹的活动，”南泽说。“但我们希望，在那些更重要的时刻之间，这能成为令人惊叹的现场音乐活动的定期心跳。”
新的演唱会系列将于9月12日美国东部时间下午5点开始(周六和周日晚些时候将重播)，Etic表示将宣布更多艺术家参加即将于19日和26日举行的演出。
note_en:
Music is about to become a much bigger part of  Fortnite. Today, Epic is announcing a new, three-week-long concert series that will take place on the game’s party royale island. It kicks off with a live performance by Dominic Fike on September 12th and will be broadcast from a brand-new Los Angeles studio built specifically for in-game concerts. It’s all part of a bigger plan to turn  Fortnite’s virtual stage into an important place for musicians.
 “This is a tour stop,” explains Nate Nanzer,  Fortnite’s head of global partnerships. “If you’re on tour, you want to stop on the  Fortnite stage. It’s a unique way to get in front of an audience that maybe you’re not reaching through other means.”
 “If you’re on tour, you want to stop on the ‘Fortnite’ stage.”
 Epic has been building up to this for a while. The developer first launched party royale — a separate, violence-free island within  Fortnite —  back in April, and it features both a concert stage and a theater space, among other attractions. So far the likes of Diplo, Steve Aoki, Deadmau5, and Kenshi Yonezu have performed music, while the theater has been used to screen  a  Tenet trailer,  Epic’s Apple-mocking short “Nineteen Eighty-Fortnite,” and even  a discussion about race in America. “They’ve been performing really well, we’ve seen millions of people come and engage with these different shows that have happened to date in party royale,” Nanzer says. “So we’ve been looking at that and figuring out how we can do this more often.”
 To do that, Epic built what it describes as a state-of-the-art studio space in LA. It has a massive LED wall and floor and robocams that can be operated remotely. (Nanzer says that much of the space was designed around COVID protocols. “We’ve thought through all of that.”) While past events were successful, they were also limited primarily to DJs performing in their own homes. The studio is meant to make performances bigger and more impressive, starting with Fike. “This will be a full live concert broadcast into the game,” says Nanzer. “He’s going to be playing with his band, and he’s going to be playing his new music live for the first time.”
   In the future, the team at Epic is hoping use the stage to utilize mixed and augmented reality effects. (Helpfully, Epic also makes the Unreal Engine, which can be used to build these kinds of experiences.) Nanzer also says that Epic is looking at making these events longer. Many of the early party royale concerts lasted 10-15 minutes, but that might not be the case as the venue evolves. “You might see 40-45 minutes, maybe an hour, maybe even more for some acts that have a bigger catalog,” he explains.
 The ultimate goal is to turn  Fornite’s virtual venue into one that becomes an integral part of the music scene. “It’s something that we’re thinking about as a platform for artists,” Nanzer says. “Similar to the way if you’re an artist and you have a new album coming out, you might play on  Fallon or  Saturday Night Live, or some platform like that. We think this is a similar platform where you can get in front of the 350 million plus  Fortnite players.”
 The existence of this concert series isn’t expected to have an impact on  Fortnite’s big musical events, like those from  Marshmello and  Travis Scott. Those lure in  tens of millions of players, but they also take a long time to put together — there was a full year in between the two events. The party royale concerts, meanwhile, are meant to be smaller-scale shows that can help fill in those long gaps. “I imagine we’ll continue to do amazing events in the future as well,” says Nanzer. “But we want this to be a regular heartbeat of awesome live music events, between those bigger moments in time.”
 The new concert series kicks off on September 12th at 5PM ET (it will be rebroadcast later on Saturday and Sunday), and Epic says that it will be announcing more artists for upcoming shows on the 19th and 26th.
title: EPIC推出了为期三周的堡垒之夜系列演唱会，从9月1日开始。12，它将在洛杉矶一个专门为现场表演配备的新演播室播出
title_en: Epic unveils a three week concert series on Fortnite, starting Sept. 12, which will broadcast from a new LA studio specially outfitted for live performances

note:
Yubio宣布推出新版本的配备USB-C的YubiKey5安全密钥，内置NFC，理论上应该是一个方便的组合，并与许多未来的设备兼容。
之前，YubiKey提供了YubiKey 5系列，您可以选择USB-A、USB-A和NFC、USB-C和USB-C以及USB-C和Lightning。但是，有了今天发布的新YubiKey 5C NFC，你不再需要在能够将钥匙插入USB-C端口或NFC身份验证的便利性之间做出选择，NFC身份验证让你只需在设备上轻敲钥匙-你现在可以购买一把同时提供这两种功能的钥匙。
基于硬件的密钥要求您在尝试登录时插入密钥或轻触设备，从而提供额外的帐户安全保护。这使得它们成为基于短信的双因素身份验证的一个很好的替代方案，与来自Authy等身份验证应用程序的安全密钥或代码相比，黑客可以更容易地拦截这些身份验证。
YubiKey 5C NFC今天上市，售价55美元，比标准的YubiKey 5C贵了5美元，Yubio称其支持iOS、Android、Windows、MacOS和Linux。如果您确实购买了安全密钥，请查看我们的指南，了解如何使用它。
note_en:
Yubico is announcing a new version of its USB-C equipped YubiKey 5 security key with NFC built in, which should theoretically be a convenient combo and compatible with many future devices moving forward.
 Previously, YubiKey offered the YubiKey 5 series in your choice of USB-A; USB-A and NFC; USB-C; and  USB-C and Lightning. But with the new YubiKey 5C NFC that’s being announced today, you no longer have to pick between being able to plug your key into a USB-C port  or the convenience of NFC authentication, which lets you just tap your key on your device — you can now buy a key that offers both.
  Hardware-based keys offer an extra layer of account security by requiring that you either plug in the key or tap it to your device when you’re trying to log in. That makes them a good alternative to text message-based two-factor authentication, which can be intercepted by hackers more easily than things like security keys or codes from an authenticator app like Authy.
 The YubiKey 5C NFC is available today and costs $55, which is $5 more than the standard YubiKey 5C, and Yubico says it supports iOS, Android, Windows, macOS and Linux. And if you do invest in a security key, check out our guide about  how to use it.
title: Yubio推出了带有USB-C和NFC的YubiKey 5C，并支持iOS、Android、Windows、MacOS和Linux，今天的售价为55美元
title_en: Yubico launches YubiKey 5C with USB-C and NFC, and support for iOS, Android, Windows, macOS, and Linux, available today for $55

note: Octopus Deploy是适用于AWS、Azure、.NET、Java、Kubernetes、Windows和Linux的部署和操作工具，以及Kubernetes YAML生成器
note_en: Octopus Deploy is a Deployment and Operations tool for AWS, Azure, .NET, Java, Kubernetes, Windows and Linux, and a Kubernetes YAML generator
title: Kubernetes YAML生成器
